home.intro: I've been building Android apps since 2012 — and the craft still excites me every single day. From fintech to wellness, I bring technical depth and a genuine love for creating apps that people enjoy using.

about.title: Hello,
about.bio1: Started building for Android over 13 years ago. Since then I've shipped across <highlight>fintech, e-commerce, health and retail</highlight> — at companies like Thoughtworks, Magazine Luiza and XP — always focused on scalable apps and hard technical problems.
about.bio2: Beyond coding, I like to <highlight>understand the product deeply and bring suggestions</highlight> — ownership matters to me, not just delivery. I also invest in people: mentoring, driving architecture decisions and pushing quality as a team habit.
about.bio3: My toolkit centres on <highlight>Kotlin, Jetpack Compose, Flutter and KMP</highlight> — but I care more about choosing the right tool than stacking acronyms. Good engineering is mostly clear thinking, honest tradeoffs and code the next person can actually read.
about.skillListeningDesc: I ask before I assume. Understanding context is half the solution.
about.skillCollaborationDesc: Code reviews, pairing, knowledge sharing — I build with the team, not beside it.
about.skillMentoringDesc: I invest in the people around me. Their growth is part of my job.
about.skillDetailDesc: Craft matters at every layer, from API contract to pixel alignment.
about.skillResilienceDesc: I ship under pressure and treat setbacks as feedback, not failure.

descriptions.1: Modularized Android app for searching and playing 30-second music previews via the iTunes API. Features MVVM + Clean Architecture, Retrofit, RxAndroid, Room for local persistence, Dagger DI, and a custom Service for audio playback. Includes favorites, custom playlists and Dark/Light theme.
descriptions.2: Android app to consume the FutMarket API, displaying football market data.
descriptions.3: Flutter app displaying characters and episodes from the Rick and Morty universe.
descriptions.4: Android test project for Reign Softwarehouse, showcasing Clean Architecture on Android.

contact.title: Let's talk 🤙
contact.cvLabel: Get a copy of my CV:
contact.quote: "The future belongs to those who believe in the beauty of their dreams." – Eleanor Roosevelt

recommendation.title: What my colleagues say about me

messages.yris: "A very focused and critical person, and I believe always very attentive to details. We get along very well at work and I think it is very easy to work with you, no drama and everything straight to the point."
messages.fabio: "Dedication, focus, delivery and relevant questions. I admire your work and I hope you continue seeking to grow."
messages.jessica: "Mastery of Android and has an analytical profile, always pays attention to details before acting. Also very proactive, anticipates situations and brings solutions."
messages.bruna: "Practical and objective. Communicates well and clearly. Has an excellent analytical profile, making good analyses and investigations. Documents things with quality. Has a lot of autonomy and proactivity."
messages.murilo: "Jean is a very competent Android developer, with above-average technical mastery. Reliable, with quality and punctual deliveries, has a calm demeanor that makes the day-to-day easier."
messages.weslley: "Professional, responsible, visionary. Always keep being open to new things. That will always take you to higher levels."

thoughtworks.h1: Leading the re-platforming of the Breathwrk Android app from the ground up, defining architecture and the modern technology stack.
thoughtworks.h2: Establishing engineering standards and best practices for the development team to ensure a scalable and maintainable codebase.
thoughtworks.h3: Applying deep knowledge of the Android ecosystem to deliver a high-performance wellness application.

magalu.h1: Led the refactoring and development of a new advertisement SDK, enabling scalable ad management for internal and external use cases.
magalu.h2: Contributed to significant performance enhancements and bug fixes across the ecosystem, including the Netshoes and Magalu apps.
magalu.h3: Actively monitored app performance and release stability using Firebase and Datadog, ensuring high quality for apps like Netshoes and Magalu.

xp.h1: Played a proactive role in architectural improvements and introducing new features across cross-squad environments.
xp.h2: Actively mentored junior developers, accelerating their technical growth and onboarding in Android development.
xp.h3: Managed the release cycle for major applications (XP, Rico, and Clear) and led War-Rooms to solve critical production issues.

nttdata.h1: Part of the team responsible for capturing and tracking user behavior to drive data-informed business decisions.
nttdata.h2: Significantly improved data quality and accuracy, ensuring reliable tracking of user journeys across the app.
nttdata.h3: Focused on fixing critical bugs and optimizing performance for high-traffic fintech and logistics solutions.

hubxp.h1: Developed and implemented advanced geolocation features for healthcare and education platforms.
hubxp.h2: Served as a key member of a specialized support squad, addressing on-demand tickets and production-critical issues with high agility.
hubxp.h3: Provided guidance to modernize legacy codebases and improve the overall stability and quality of Android applications.

omnichat.h1: Served as the sole Android developer, taking full ownership of designing and building the Android version of the company's main product.
omnichat.h2: Established the Android architecture, data persistence (SQLite) and client-server communication (REST/JSON) layer from scratch.
omnichat.h3: Gained end-to-end product responsibility — from architecture decisions to release — in a fast-paced startup environment.

snowmanlabs.h1: Worked as an embedded consultant delivering mobile solutions for external clients alongside designers, architects and developers.
snowmanlabs.h2: Developed end-to-end Android features in a collaborative, client-facing environment with high-quality delivery expectations.
snowmanlabs.h3: Operated in a consultancy model that demanded adaptability, rapid onboarding to client contexts and clear communication.

totvs.h1: Contributed to FLUIG, a productivity and collaboration platform published on the Google Play Store and used by enterprise clients across Brazil.
totvs.h2: Worked within a structured mobile team on a large-scale ERP product, following Kanban methodology and pair programming practices.
totvs.h3: Handled legacy bug fixes and participated in new feature delivery for one of Brazil's largest enterprise software companies.

sabbatical.h1: Left Brazil in 2017 and spent 4 years backpacking through South America — visiting Uruguay, Argentina, Chile and the Patagonia region.
sabbatical.h2: Maintained remote freelance Android development for Brazilian startups and small businesses throughout the journey.
sabbatical.h3: Returned with a broader perspective on people, cultures and resilience — experiences that shaped a more empathetic and self-directed approach to work.

softin.h1: Started professional career in web development (.NET/C#) then transitioned to Android — building the technical foundation for over a decade of mobile work.
softin.h2: Built and shipped Android apps in Java with SQLite and REST/JSON, including SimpsAndroid, a mobile integration system for enterprise clients.
softin.h3: Practiced TDD, pair programming and code review from day one, building engineering habits that carried through the entire career.

footer.developedBy: Developed with ❤ by Jean Paulo de Almeida Silva
